ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee. */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the employee. */,
  "title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee. */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee. */,
  "twitter_handle" TEXT /* Twitter handle of the employee. */,
  "telephone_numbers" TEXT /* Telephone number of the employee. */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the company the employee works for. */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the employee belongs to. */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee. */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired. */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date the employee left the company (if applicable). */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's manager. */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact. */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact. */,
  "address" TEXT /* Home address of the employee. */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary of the employee. */,
  "benefits" TEXT /* List of benefits the employee is entitled to. */,
  "performance_reviews" TEXT /* List of performance reviews. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company. */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company. */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry sector of the company. */,
  "founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the company was founded. */,
  "ceo" TEXT /* Name of the CEO. */,
  "website" TEXT /* Company's website. */,
  "employee_count" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the company. */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* Location of the company's headquarters. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department. */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department. */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the department. */,
  "department_head" INTEGER /* ID of the department head. */,
  "established_date" TEXT /* Date the department was established. */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the department's role and responsibilities. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_department_head FOREIGN KEY ("department_head") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "communication_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each communication method. */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the communication method (e.g., Email, Twitter). */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the method. */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the communication method. */,
  "is_enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the communication method is currently enabled or disabled. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_communication" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee. */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* ID of the communication method. */,
  "preferred" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the communication method is the employee's preferred method. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "method_id")
)
column_names: Reference to the company the employee works for.
Reference to the department the employee belongs to.
Date of birth of the employee.
Date the employee left the company (if applicable).
Phone number of the emergency contact.
List of benefits the employee is entitled to.
Number of employees in the company.
Brief description of the department's role and responsibilities.
Unique identifier for each communication method.
Name of the communication method (e.g., Email, Twitter).
Priority level of the communication method.
Indicates whether the communication method is currently enabled or disabled.
Indicates whether the communication method is the employee's preferred method.
